app.title: Work Style Test | Workplace Personality - DopaBrain
app.description: Discover your work style! Leader, Executor, Creator, Analyst, or Connector — find out which type you are in just 25 questions.

intro.title:
Work Style
Test
intro.subtitle:
What type of person are you at work?
25 workplace scenarios reveal your true work style!

Work Style Test Result: {{type}} ({{enName}})
What's your work type?

questions.q1: When starting a team project, I...
questions.q1a: Set the overall direction and goals first
questions.q1b: Start immediately with what can be done now
questions.q1d: Analyze data and past examples first
questions.q2a: Organize the agenda and lead the discussion
questions.q2e: Make sure everyone gets to speak
questions.q3: When facing a tight deadline, I...
questions.q3a: Prioritize tasks and rally the team
questions.q3b: Work extra hours to solve it personally
questions.q3c: Improvise a faster solution on the spot
questions.q4: When I come up with a new project idea, I...
questions.q4a: Connect it to the team's overall direction
questions.q4b: Try it out small-scale right away
questions.q4c: Develop it into something more original
questions.q4e: Refine the idea together with colleagues
questions.q5: The moment I feel most rewarded at work is...
questions.q5a: When the team achieves its goal
questions.q5b: When I complete my assigned tasks
questions.q5c: When my idea actually gets used
questions.q5d: When I find the answer to a complex problem
questions.q5e: When a colleague gains strength because of me
questions.q6: When conflict arises in the team, I...
questions.q6b: Quickly move to implement a solution
questions.q6c: Reframe the problem from a new angle
questions.q6e: Listen to both sides and empathize
questions.q7a: Goals and plans visible at a glance
questions.q7b: A practical space with only essentials
questions.q7c: Full of things that inspire me
questions.q7e: Photos of colleagues and shared memories
questions.q8a: Start by structuring the entire storyline
questions.q8b: Include only the key points, concisely
questions.q8d: Include plenty of data and evidence
questions.q8e: Tailor it to the audience's level
questions.q9: When I encounter something I don't know, I...
questions.q9d: Research in depth and organize systematically
questions.q10: My way of recharging on weekends is...
questions.q10d: Reading or taking courses to gain knowledge
questions.q10e: Spending time with family and friends
questions.q11: When a teammate makes a mistake, I...
questions.q11a: Create a system to prevent recurrence
questions.q11b: Fix it quickly and move on
questions.q11c: Reframe it as a learning opportunity
questions.q11d: Identify the cause and give specific feedback
questions.q12: The hardest moment at work for me is...
questions.q12b: When results don't come despite hard work
questions.q12c: When only rigid methods are allowed
questions.q12d: When decisions are made on gut feeling without logic
questions.q13: What I value most in a performance review is...
questions.q14: When a new hire joins the team, I...
questions.q14a: Share the organizational culture and goals first
questions.q14c: Ask them to bring a fresh perspective
questions.q14e: Approach warmly to help them adapt quickly
questions.q15: When a major change happens at the company, I...
questions.q15a: Clearly communicate the new direction to my team
questions.q15c: Think of ways to leverage the change as opportunity
questions.q15d: Carefully analyze the impact of the change
questions.q15e: Support teammates in adapting to the change
questions.q16: My ideal manager is someone who...
questions.q16a: Provides a clear vision and direction
questions.q16b: Gives autonomy and evaluates by results
questions.q16e: Cares about individual growth and feelings
questions.q17a: Have a strategic lunch with key people
questions.q17b: Eat quickly and do more work
questions.q17c: Try a new restaurant or menu
questions.q18: When a project seems to be going wrong, I...
questions.q18a: Gather the team and redefine the direction
questions.q18b: Take immediate action to fix the problem
questions.q18d: Analyze the root cause of the problem
questions.q18e: Boost team morale and find a breakthrough together
questions.q20b: Essentials only, as short as possible
questions.q21: When assigned to plan a team event, I...
questions.q21a: Create a meaningful occasion tied to team goals
questions.q21d: Carefully compare budget and venue options
questions.q21e: Find out everyone's preferences so all can enjoy
questions.q22: When a colleague shares their worries with me, I...
questions.q22a: Suggest solutions and a next direction
questions.q22b: Draft a specific action plan together
questions.q22d: Help them analyze the root cause logically
questions.q23: The most enjoyable moment while working is...
questions.q23a: When the team delivers results under my leadership
questions.q23b: Checking off the last item on my to-do list
questions.q23e: A moment of real connection with a teammate
questions.q24: Remote work vs. office — which do you prefer?
questions.q24a: Either works — I choose strategically based on the situation
questions.q24b: Remote work where I can focus without interruptions
questions.q24c: Remote work in a free environment
questions.q24d: Office with a quiet personal space
questions.q24e: Office where I can be with my teammates
questions.q25a: A leader running a team or organization
questions.q25b: A top expert recognized in my field
questions.q25c: A creator who built something innovative
questions.q25d: An authority with deep expertise in my domain
questions.q25e: A mentor who positively influenced many people

L.name: The Leader
L.subtitle: Sets the direction and leads the team
L.description: You are a natural direction-setter. Even in complex situations, you have a clear vision and the ability to lead teams with confidence. You make decisions quickly, take responsibility seriously, and view the team's success as your own. You excel at connecting organizational goals to individual roles, and people naturally gravitate toward you. Even in uncertainty, your confident demeanor gives the team a sense of stability.
L.strength1: Strategic thinking — seeing the big picture and setting direction
L.strength2: Leadership to motivate teams and drive results
L.weakness1: May overlook details or implementation steps
L.weakness2: Can lack communication by trying to handle everything alone
L.bestMatchReason: The Executor turns your strategy into reality, fast and effectively. Leader's direction + Executor's drive = ultimate combination!
L.motivation: You feel most energized when the team moves toward a shared goal under your direction. You experience both the responsibility and fulfillment of leadership in creating results together.

E.name: The Executor
E.subtitle: Actions speak louder — the results-maker
E.description: You are a performance machine who shows results through action, not words. You display remarkable focus and drive in achieving any given goal. Rather than overthinking, you concentrate on what can be done right now and prove your value through deliverables. You find the shortest path to completing any task and are an indispensable contributor to any team. Turning ideas into reality is precisely your role.
E.strength2: High productivity and task completion ability
E.strength3: Master of self-management and time efficiency
E.weakness1: Focusing on immediate tasks may cause you to lose sight of the bigger picture
E.weakness2: Prioritizing results over process can create friction with teammates
E.bestMatchReason: When the Creator's fresh ideas meet your execution power, the best results are born. The dream partnership for turning ideas into reality!
E.motivation: You feel the greatest satisfaction when checking off the last item on your to-do list, or when you confirm the achievement of a goal. Concrete, measurable results are your source of energy.

C.name: The Creator
C.subtitle: Solves problems with bold new ideas
C.description: You are an innovator who breaks existing molds and opens new possibilities. You find ideas where others cannot see them and redefine problems through creative solutions. You don't fear change — you embrace it — and provide fresh stimulation and inspiration to your organization. Teams with you ask 'How can we make it work?' rather than 'Why won't it work?' Turning the impossible into the possible with imagination and an experimental spirit is your specialty.
C.strength3: Ability to redefine problems from a fresh perspective
C.weakness1: May struggle to translate ideas into execution
C.weakness2: Chasing novelty may weaken follow-through on finishing tasks
C.bestMatchReason: The Analyst adds logical grounds and feasibility to your creative ideas. Creativity + Analysis = Innovation Complete!
C.motivation: You feel the greatest excitement when ideas in your head come to life as real products or campaigns, or when you propose a solution no one else thought of. A free environment and creative challenges keep you alive.

A.name: The Analyst
A.subtitle: Finds the best answers through data and logic
A.description: You are a precise expert who finds the best answer through data and logic. You trust evidence over instinct and analysis over guesswork, and the more complex the problem, the more you shine. Your thorough, systematic approach minimizes errors and produces optimal results. Your analysis plays a decisive role in helping teams make the right decisions. As someone who never misses even the smallest detail, you are the team's reliable safety net.
A.strength1: Outstanding analytical skill and critical thinking
A.weakness2: Focusing on data may cause you to overlook the human element
A.bestMatchReason: Adding your analytical grounding to the Creator's ideas produces realizable innovation. The perfect harmony of logic and imagination!
A.motivation: You feel deep satisfaction when you solve a complex puzzle-like problem logically, or when data points clearly to the right answer. The process of pursuing accuracy and perfection is itself a pleasure for you.

R.name: The Connector
R.subtitle: Creates team culture and drives collaboration
R.description: You are the key figure who connects people and manages the team's energy. With exceptional empathy, you understand teammates' emotions, mediate conflicts, and foster collaboration. Teams with you become a truly cohesive unit, not just a group of workers. Creating an environment where people feel safe to speak freely is your specialty, and you are responsible for the team's psychological safety. You instinctively know that team success starts with relationships.
R.strength2: Ability to maintain a positive team atmosphere
R.weakness1: May be overly influenced by others' opinions, destabilizing your own decisions
R.weakness2: May find it hard to assert your own position in conflict situations
R.bestMatchReason: With the Leader's clear direction plus your relationship skills, the entire team becomes one. Vision + Communication = Strongest Team!
R.motivation: You feel the greatest fulfillment when a colleague says they gained strength because of you, or when you successfully mediate a conflict and the team unites again. Connection and trust between people is your driving force.
